hero.desc: Python developer focused on data pipelines, ETL infrastructure, and backend architecture. Based in Montevideo, Uruguay. Open to interesting problems.
about.heading: About
about.name:
Emiliano
G.O.
about.bio.1: Backend developer and data scientist. I work mostly in Python: pipelines, APIs, and the infrastructure that keeps them running. I care about clean architecture, good documentation, and building things that hold up under load.
about.bio.2: I learn by building things until they break, then figuring out why. Also run LibreCourseUY, an open source organization promoting the OSS ecosystem in Uruguay.
about.blog.desc: Thoughts on backend, data, and building things.
projects.heading: Projects
projects.dbwarden.desc: DBWarden is a SQL-first migration system for SQLAlchemy that replaces Python-based migration workflows with explicit, reviewable SQL generated directly from your models. No migration runtime, no generated Python scripts. Built-in rollback, schema snapshots, offline CI generation, pre-deploy impact analysis, multi-database support, and a full CLI for managing the complete migration lifecycle from init through production rollbacks.
projects.schemap.desc: Automatic Pydantic v2 schemas from SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM models.
projects.vigil.desc: Personal GitHub activity monitoring workflow built with Prefect. Fetches events, commits, and PR activity, then stores structured records in ClickHouse.
librecourse.heading: LibreCourseUY
librecourse.title: LibreCourseUY
librecourse.desc.1: An open source organization focused on promoting the FOSS ecosystem in Uruguay. We build and maintain free tools, services, and infrastructure for the Uruguayan developer community, and anyone who wants to contribute.
librecourse.desc.2: What started as a small side project to help some friends grew into a 30-person operation with hundreds of members, all focused on learning, growing together, and building useful projects for our country.

studies.career.1.resp.1: Studying advanced statistical methods: inference, regression, multivariate analysis, and stochastic processes
studies.career.1.resp.2: Applying computational tools: R, Python, SQL for data analysis and statistical modeling

studies.career.1.resp.5: Integrating technology-oriented approaches to solve quantitative problems across domains

studies.career.2.resp.2: Building software systems with emphasis on architecture, scalability, and reliability

studies.career.2.resp.5: Developing low-level systems knowledge including operating systems and compilers

studies.edu.2.desc: Intensive data analytics program covering the full analytics workflow: data extraction, cleaning, exploration, visualization, and insight communication using industry-standard tools.

oss.heading: Open Source
oss.desc.1: Batteries-included async FastAPI starter shipping auth (sessions, OAuth, API keys), FastCRUD, Alembic, Taskiq workers, Redis caching, and per-tier rate limiting out of the box, organized as a uv workspace with a vertical-slice module layout. The bp CLI handles Docker Compose generation, env auditing, and third-party plugin mounting, making it the open-source foundation FastroAI extends for full SaaS builds. Contributor to documentation and project maintenance.
oss.desc.2: I focus on documentation quality and keeping the project ergonomic for people building real FastAPI services. Named co-maintainer in
oss.desc.3: My contributions lean toward developer experience: cleaner abstractions, lower boilerplate, and making the codebase easier to extend. Committed to a contribution surface where adding new things stays seamless.

projects.page.title: Projects | Emiliano G.O.
projects.page.desc: DBWarden, schemap, and other Python packages — database tooling, ETL utilities, and open source projects by Emiliano Gandini Outeda.
projects.dbwarden.subtitle: The SQL-first database toolkit for SQLAlchemy.
projects.dbwarden.detail: DBWarden is a SQL-first migration system for SQLAlchemy that replaces Python-based migration workflows with explicit, reviewable SQL generated directly from your models. Unlike script-based migration tools, DBWarden does not introduce a migration runtime or require executing generated Python migration code. It produces plain SQL files that can be reviewed, versioned, and executed directly against any environment.
projects.feature.sqlfirst.desc: Migrations are plain SQL. No runtime required.
projects.feature.rollback.desc: Every migration carries upgrade + rollback SQL.
projects.feature.snapshot.desc: Checksummed JSON after every migration for deterministic diffs.
projects.feature.diffing.desc: Type, nullability, default, and comment changes.
projects.feature.impact.desc: AST-based scan to detect breaking changes before deploy.
projects.feature.offline.desc: Export model state, generate migrations anywhere, no DB needed.
projects.feature.fastapi.desc: Startup validation, health checks, runtime lifecycle.
projects.schemap.detail.1: Automatic Pydantic v2 schemas from SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM models. Define your model once, schemap generates Create, Update, Public, and Full schemas automatically, with field-level type mapping, nullability handling, and configurable exclusions.
projects.schemap.detail.2: Supports AutoBase inheritance and @auto_schema decorator, with built-in TimestampMixin and SoftDeleteMixin. Eliminates repetitive schema boilerplate.
projects.pypi.heading: PyPI Utilities
projects.seoslug.subtitle: Deterministic SEO payload generation for Python.
projects.seoslug.detail: seoslug is a deterministic, pure-function SEO toolkit for Python that generates complete SEO payloads from two typed inputs. Define your site once with SEOConfig (canonical host, site name, title template, base URL) and your page with SEOEntity (entity type, title, excerpt, image, timestamps). The single build_seo_payload() function merges them into a structured dict that maps directly to canonical URLs, Open Graph tags, Twitter Cards, and JSON-LD structured data.
projects.seoslug.desc: Deterministic SEO payload generation: canonical URLs, Open Graph, Twitter Cards, and JSON-LD. Same input always produces the same output. Testable, cacheable, predictable.
projects.detrack.desc: Strip tracking parameters from URLs. Deterministically. Zero dependencies. 60+ default patterns, case-insensitive, pure functions, metadata returned.
projects.acrresolv.desc: Acronym resolver for Python. Converts natural language phrases to acronyms. Zero dependencies, CLI and library usage. Being expanded with ML.
projects.more.heading: More Projects
projects.more.crxml.desc: Fast streaming parser for Crystal Reports XML exports with Rust acceleration. Streams row-by-row without loading the full document into memory, handling files of any size. Built-in pipeline stages: rename, cast types, filter, and drop fields, all chained with pipe operators for composable transformations. Parallel mode distributes batches across worker processes using Python's multiprocessing, achieving near-linear speedups on multi-core systems. Outputs DataFrames via pandas, CSV files, or lists of dicts. The pandas import is lazy, only loaded when you actually convert, keeping the CLI lightweight.
projects.more.vigil.desc: Personal GitHub activity monitoring workflow built with Prefect. Fetches events, commits, and PR activity via PyGithub, then stores structured records in ClickHouse through dbwarden's migration framework. Designed for automated periodic execution: the flow runs on a cron schedule, collects the latest activity since the last run, and inserts it into the warehouse. Structured logging captures each pipeline stage, and errors surface through Prefect's native notification system instead of failing silently. A simple dashboard query gives you a timeline of your open source contribution activity without relying on GitHub's own graphs.
projects.more.mapcreator.desc: Tile-based building map editor built with React and Canvas. Draw multi-floor building layouts with a grid-based editor; place walls, designate rooms, and connect them with doors, stairs, and elevators. Each floor is a separate layer, and the A* pathfinding system can route from any room to any other across floors, automatically selecting stairs or elevators. The canvas renders at 60fps with virtual scrolling for large maps. State is managed entirely through React hooks, making it easy to undo, redo, or export the map data as JSON for use in other tools. No external mapping libraries or APIs required.
